skill.name: zone-map-prescription
skill.purpose:
Generate variable-rate seeding prescriptions based on drainage-stratified yield response data. Produces a downloadable ISOXML file compatible with John Deere Operations Center and other ISO 11783 equipment.
Use when: grower asks about variable-rate seeding, wants to optimize populations by field/zone, or needs a prescription file for their planter.

interpretation.stage:
Recommended rates are derived from the DATA — the population bucket with the highest weighted-average yield per drainage class. This is empirical, not theoretical. If the data shows peak yield at 35K for well-drained, that's what the prescription uses.
Zones with rate change less than 500 seeds/ac (corn) or 3,000 seeds/ac (soybeans) are treated as "on target" — no meaningful adjustment needed.
The ISOXML file contains per-zone rates. When loaded into the planter display, each zone gets its own seeding rate.

skill.communication:
Present the prescription as actionable: "I've generated a variable-rate prescription for your [crop] based on how each drainage class responds to different populations in your data. [X] of [Y] zones have recommended rate changes. The ISOXML file is ready to download and load into your planter display."
If most zones show no change, frame positively: "Your current rates are well-matched to your fields. Only [X] zones have meaningful adjustments."
Never present drainage-stratified rates as universal truth — they're derived from THIS operation's data. Different operations may peak at different populations.
skill.follow_up: After prescription generation, suggest: "Want me to run the YP1K analysis to see which hybrids have the most room for rate adjustment? That would give us the full picture — hybrid-level recommendations on top of the zone-level prescription."
